scriptName: VS Code Folder-Only Context Menu Repair
description: Removes file/background context menu entries and keeps only the folder entry, then restarts Explorer.

messages.scriptDisabled: Folder-only repair is disabled in config.
messages.notAdmin: This script must be run as Administrator.
messages.removingTarget: Removing context menu entry on target '{target}': {path}
messages.restartingExplorer: Restarting explorer.exe so context menu refreshes...
messages.refreshingShell: Refreshing Windows shell (minimum components, no explorer.exe restart)...
messages.refreshFullRestart: Falling back to full explorer.exe restart (--restart flag set)...
messages.refreshDone: Shell refresh completed -- right-click menus should now reflect the repair.
messages.refreshNothingSelected: Refresh aborted -- all notifications disabled. Enable at least one of --assoc / --broadcast / --restart.
messages.verifyHandlersHeader: VS Code context menu handler verification:
messages.verifyHandlersTargetSkip: [SKIP] {target} -- not in scope for this repair (path: {path})
messages.verifyHandlersOverallPass: Overall: PASS -- all expected handlers are registered.
messages.verifyHandlersOverallFail: Overall: FAIL -- {failed} handler(s) missing or invalid out of {total} checked.
messages.verifyHandlersStart: Verifying VS Code context menu handlers are registered...
messages.verifyReportFailed: FAILED to write verification report at {path} -- {error}
messages.verifyReportSkipped: Verification report skipped (use --report to enable on standalone runs).
messages.verifyRepairTriggered: Pass 1 verification FAILED -- --verify-repair is on, attempting auto-recovery...
messages.verifyRepairRetryPlan: Pass 2 plan: refresh with --both (SHChangeNotify + WM_SETTINGCHANGE) AND --restart (kill+relaunch explorer.exe), then re-verify.
messages.verifyRepairSecondVerify: Pass 2 refresh complete -- running second verification...
messages.verifyRepairRecovered: Pass 2 verification PASSED -- handlers recovered after auto-repair.
messages.verifyRepairStillFailing: Pass 2 verification still FAILED -- manual intervention required. See verify-reports/ for both passes.
messages.done: Folder-only context menu repair completed successfully.
messages.completedWithWarnings: Repair completed with warnings -- check output above.
messages.rollbackBanner: VS Code Folder-Only Repair -- ROLLBACK (restore default entries)
messages.rollbackStart: Restoring default VS Code context menu entries on all three targets (file, directory, background).
messages.rollbackMissingTemplate: No defaultCommandTemplates.{target} entry for edition '{edition}' -- skipping target.
messages.rollbackVerify: Verifying restored state (all three targets must be present)...
messages.rollbackDone: Rollback completed successfully -- default entries restored on file, directory, and background.
messages.rollbackPartial: Rollback completed with warnings -- one or more targets did not restore. See errors above.
messages.rollbackExeMissing: Cannot restore edition '{edition}' -- VS Code executable not found. Skipping all targets for this edition.

commands.dry-run: Preview repair (no registry writes, no snapshots)
commands.no-restart: Repair but do NOT restart explorer.exe
commands.verify: Verify final state without changing anything (dry-run + trace)
commands.restore: Re-import newest BEFORE snapshot for the chosen edition
commands.rollback: Restore DEFAULT installer entries on all 3 targets (inverse of repair)
commands.refresh: Lightweight shell refresh after a repair. Run '.\run.ps1 refresh --help' for the full per-step / per-flag breakdown.
commands.verify-handlers: Check whether VS Code context menu handlers are registered. Prints PASS/FAIL per target. Read-only.

examples: .\run.ps1 restore                                    # undo via newest BEFORE snapshot
.\run.ps1 refresh                                    # minimum-components shell refresh (no explorer.exe kill)
.\run.ps1 refresh --both                             # explicit: both notifications (same as default)
.\run.ps1 refresh --restart                          # minimum refresh PLUS full explorer.exe restart fallback
.\run.ps1 refresh --verify                           # refresh, then check handlers and print PASS/FAIL
.\run.ps1 refresh --verify -Edition stable          # refresh + verify, scoped, JSON report saved
.\run.ps1 refresh --verify-repair                    # verify, and on FAIL auto-rerun --both --restart then re-verify
.\run.ps1 verify-handlers                            # standalone PASS/FAIL check (no writes, no refresh)
.\run.ps1 verify-handlers -Edition stable            # check only the stable edition
.\run.ps1 verify-handlers --report                   # ALSO save JSON troubleshooting report

refresh.title: refresh -- lightweight Windows shell refresh after a context-menu repair
refresh.summary: Tells Explorer + every top-level window to reload shell associations and settings WITHOUT killing explorer.exe. Use this right after 'repair' so right-click menus update immediately. Each step is independent and controlled by its own flag.

steps.name: 1. SHChangeNotify(SHCNE_ASSOCCHANGED)
steps.what: Calls shell32!SHChangeNotify with event=0x08000000, flags=SHCNF_IDLIST. Tells the Windows shell to flush its cached file-type / progid / verb associations -- the cache that drives the right-click menu.
steps.when: Run this after ADDING, REMOVING or RE-LABELING any HKCR\*\shell\..., HKCR\Directory\shell\..., or HKCR\Directory\Background\shell\... entry. Without it the old menu can stay cached for hours.
steps.enabledBy: ON by default. Use '--assoc-only' to send ONLY this step. Use '--broadcast-only' to skip it.
steps.sideEffects: None. No process is killed, no window flicker.

steps.name: 2. WM_SETTINGCHANGE broadcast (lParam='Environment')
steps.what: Calls user32!SendMessageTimeout(HWND_BROADCAST=0xFFFF, WM_SETTINGCHANGE=0x001A, 0, 'Environment', SMTO_ABORTIFHUNG, 5000ms). Nudges every top-level window to re-read environment variables and shell settings.
steps.when: Useful when the repair also touched PATH, file associations, or per-user shell settings, or when third-party shell extensions cache state per-window.
steps.enabledBy: ON by default. Use '--broadcast-only' to send ONLY this step. Use '--assoc-only' to skip it.
steps.sideEffects: Hung apps are skipped after the 5s timeout (SMTO_ABORTIFHUNG). No flicker, no process kill.

steps.name: 3. Restart-Explorer (full kill + relaunch)  [opt-in fallback]
steps.what: Calls Get-Process explorer | Kill, waits restartExplorerWaitMs (default 800ms), then Start-Process explorer.exe. Equivalent to the legacy hard refresh.
steps.when: Use this only when steps 1+2 are not enough -- stuck menu cache on very old Windows 10 builds, or after a corrupted edit. Closes all open Explorer windows.
steps.enabledBy: OFF by default. Add '--restart' (alias '--full') to enable.
steps.sideEffects: Closes every open Explorer window and resets the taskbar (visible flicker).

steps.name: 4. Post-refresh verification  [opt-in]
steps.what: After the refresh, runs 'verify-handlers' which queries each target key + its \command (Default) value and prints PASS/FAIL per edition/target. ALWAYS writes a timestamped JSON troubleshooting report under .resolved/52-vscode-folder-repair/verify-reports/ (plus 'verify-latest.json' for quick sharing).
steps.when: Use in CI / unattended runs to fail fast when the registry state did not match expectations.
steps.enabledBy: OFF by default. Add '--verify' to enable. Honors '-Edition' to scope the check.
steps.sideEffects: Writes JSON file(s) under .resolved/. Exit code becomes non-zero if any in-scope handler is missing or invalid.

steps.name: 5. Auto-repair on verification failure  [opt-in]
steps.what: Two-pass mode. Pass 1 runs the refresh + verify exactly as configured by your other flags. If verify FAILS, pass 2 automatically re-runs the refresh with the strongest settings (--both AND --restart, ignoring earlier --assoc-only / --broadcast-only) and verifies a second time. Both passes write separate JSON reports tagged 'pass1' and 'pass2'.
steps.when: Use in CI or first-time machine setup where you want self-healing behaviour: try the cheap refresh first, fall back to the heavy explorer kill only when needed.
steps.sideEffects: Pass 2 ALWAYS kills + relaunches explorer.exe (visible flicker). Two JSON reports per run. Exit code is 0 only if pass 2 passes.

flags.effect: Send steps 1 AND 2. This is the default; flag is accepted for clarity.
ALSO run step 3 (kill + relaunch explorer.exe). Combines with any of the above.
ALSO run step 4 (handler PASS/FAIL check) after the refresh. Always saves a JSON troubleshooting report under .resolved/52-vscode-folder-repair/verify-reports/.
Implies --verify. On verification FAIL, automatically retry refresh with --both --restart and verify again. Saves two JSON reports (pass1, pass2).
Scope step 4 to one edition: 'stable' or 'insiders'. No effect on steps 1-3.

rules: --assoc-only and --broadcast-only are mutually exclusive (the dispatcher exits 2 if both are passed).
At least one of --assoc / --broadcast / --restart must remain enabled, otherwise the refresh aborts with an error.
Exit code is 0 only when every enabled step succeeded AND (if --verify) every checked handler passed.
With --verify-repair, exit code is 0 only when pass 2 passes (or pass 1 already passed).
examples: .\run.ps1 refresh --restart --verify -Edition stable # nuke + verify only stable
.\run.ps1 refresh --verify-repair                    # cheap refresh + verify; auto-escalate to --both --restart on FAIL
.\run.ps1 refresh --assoc-only --verify-repair       # try cheapest path first, escalate only if verify fails
